TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN GENEVA COUNTY NEAR HOUSTON COUNTY BORDER ALONG REHOBETH ROAD BEFORE LIFTING AFTER CROSSING US 231 EAST OF THE NATIONAL PEANUT FESTIVAL GROUNDS. IN GENEVA COUNTY THE TORNADO DEBARKED AND DENUDED SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND SNAPPED OR UPROOTED SEVERAL MORE. DAMAGE AT THE NATIONAL PEANUT FESTIVAL GROUNDS IN HOUSTON COUNTY INCLUDED SNAPPED POWER POLES AND BUILDING DAMAGE. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF2 WITH MAX WINDS AROUND 115 MPH AND WAS 500 YARDS WIDE. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 7.6 MI.

*** 4 FATAL *** NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT THE DAMAGE AT LEONARD DRIVE IN THE REHOBETH AREAS WAS CAUSED BY STRONG STRAIGHT LINE WINDS ON THE SOUTHERN FLANK OF A TORNADO JUST TO THE NORTH. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF NUMEROUS LARGE TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. ONE MOBILE HOME WAS SPLIT BY A LARGE TREE RESULTING IN FOUR FATALITIES. DAMAGE PATTERN WAS NOT CONVERGENT AND NOTICABLY SOUTH OF THE PATH OF THE TORNADO VORTEX. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED IS AROUND 85 MPH.
*** 4 FATAL *** SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE IN THE REHOBETH AREA. GOVERNOR TWEETED 4 FATALITIES

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG E. COTTONWOOD ROAD SE OF DOTHAN AL. THE TORNADO TRAVELED EAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 2.6 MILES ALONG MCCALLISTER ROAD BEFORE LIFTING NEAR ROSEWOOD DRIVE. ONE HOME HAD ITS ROOF REMOVED WITH OTHERS HAD SIGNIFICANT SHINGLE DAMAGE. THIS...ALONG WITH OBSERVED TREE DAMAGE SUPPORTED AN EF-1 RATING WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF APPROXIMATELY 100 MPH AND MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 200 YARDS.
DOWNED TREES REPORTED NEAR THE MCCALLISTER AND BEVERLYE ROAD AREA. TDS OBSERVED ON RADAR IN THIS AREA
TREES DOWN IN THE COTTONDALE AREA
52 KNOT OR 60 MPH GUST MEASURED AT KMAI
TREES DOWN IN THE GREENWOOD AREA

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON DAMASCUS-HILTON RD JUST EAST OF ZION ROAD AND MOVED NE TOWARD CEDAR SPRINGS RD. AT THE INTERSECTION OF CEDAR SPRINGS RD AND HIGHTOWER RD, TWO RESIDENCES WERE DAMAGED, WITH A PORTION OF THE ROOF DECKS REMOVED. FARM BUILDINGS WERE ALSO DAMAGED OR DESTROYED IN THE AREA. THE TORNADO TURNED MORE TO THE EAST AND EVENTUALLY LIFTED WHEN REACHING OLD LUCILE RD AFTER A 4.5 MILE PATH. THE DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH MAX WINDS OF 115 MPH AND 400 YD WIDTH.
TREES DOWN IN EARLY COUNTY WITH DUAL-POL TDS AT THIS LOCATION

EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON GRANT GRAHAM RD CAUSING DAMAGE TO PECAN GROVES...TWO MOBILE HOMES...AND ONE SINGLE FAMILY HOME. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR APPROX. 3.75 MILES ALONG HIGHWAY 91 BEFORE LIFTING JUST SOUTH OF DONALSONVILLE. ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 90 MPH WITH A MAX WIDTH OF 600 YARDS.

EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON EARLY CO. SIDE OF EARLY-MILLER LINE NEAR THREE NOTCH ROAD. THE TORNADO MOVED NE TOWARDS US-27 NEAR BATES RD. A MOBILE HOME SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS PINES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST AND INTENSIFIED BEFORE REACHING MIDDLETON RD. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED, A FEW DEBARKED. SEVERE DAMAGE TO FARM BUILDINGS WAS NOTED. THE TORNADO LIFTED AFTER 11.1 MILES NEAR OLD DAMASCUS RD. MAX WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 115 MPH WITH A WIDTH UP TO 1000 YARDS
TREES DOWN IN EARLY COUNTY WITH DUAL POL TDS AT THIS LOCATION
SURVEY TEAM FOUND SMALL AREA OF DAMAGE TO TREES AND OUTBUILDINGS CONSISTENT WITH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF AROUND 80 MPH.
HELMS ROAD WAS IMPASSIBLE

EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 45 NORTH OF BILLY NEWBERRY ROAD. THE TORNADO LIFTTED JUST NORTH OF NEWTON RD AFTER A PATH OF 4.6 MILES. THERE WAS SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH AS WELL AS STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ON THE PERIPHERY. THE DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH MAX WINDS APPROXIMATELY 120 MPH AND MAX WIDTH OF 500 YARDS.
TREES DOWN IN EARLY COUNTY WITH DUAL-POL TDS AT THIS LOCATION

DAMAGE TO FARM EQUIPMENT DEALER AND AUTMOBILE DEALER REPORTED BY DECATUR COUNTY EMA. THE DAMAGE WAS CONCENTRATED ALONG A SMALL STRETCH OF DOTHAN ROAD IN WESTERN BAINBRIDGE. PHOTOS OF DAMAGE ANALYZED AND DETERMINED TO BE CONSISTENT WITH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS UP TO 80 MPH.

EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR ALBANY HWY AND KIMBREL RD IN EASTERN MILLER COUNTY. THE TORNADO TRAVELED NORTHEAST 10 MILES...DOING HEAVY DAMAGE TO TREES ALONG THE PATH. A HOME AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED NEAR HWY 200 IN BAKER CO. BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED NEAR 105 MPH.

NWS GROUND SURVEY INDICATES EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR FLINT RIVER JUST WEST OF US 19. NUMEROUS PECAN TREES UPROOTED AND PINE TREES SNAPPED. ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES OFF STAGE COACH ROAD. MAX WINDS ESTIMATED AT 90 TO 95 MPH. TORNADO MAY HAVE TOUCHED DOWN A BIT FURTHER WEST AND LIFTED A BIT FURTHER EAST IN INACCESSIBLE AREAS.

SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS PRODUCED A 3 TO 4 MILE SWATH OF 80 TO 85 WINDS ACROSS THE NORTHERN HALF OF ALBANY CAUSING WIDESPREAD DAMAGE ACROSS THE CITY. THE STORM SNAPPED OR UPROOTED HUNDREDS OF TREES ALONG WITH MINOR WIND DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES AND BUILDINGS. MOST OF THE SEVERE STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS DUE TO FALLING TREES. MOST INTENSE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED IN RAWSON CIRCLE AREA. ORIENTATION OF THE TREE DAMAGE ALONG WITH RADAR DAMAGE SUPPORTS DESIGNATION AS TSTM WIND DAMAGE.
LOTS OF STORM DAMAGE BEING REPORTED IN THE ALBANY AREA. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN...SOME ON HOUSES AND CARS. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
